documentation.readme: Go client to communicate with Chaos dataset API.
usage_sections: Usage: This will display help for the tool. Here are all the switches it supports. You can also set the API key as an environment variable in your bash profile.

Example: 💡 Note To run the program, you need to set the PDCPAPIKEY environment variable to your Chaos API key. 👨‍💻 Community ----- You are welcomed to join our Discord Community. You can also follow us on Twitter to keep up with everything related to chaos project. Thanks again for your c

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the recon category. Fits when the engagement needs: Go client to communicate with Chaos dataset API.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Go client to communicate with Chaos dataset API.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: go install -v github.com/projectdiscovery/chaos-client/cmd/chaos@latest. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `go install -v github.com/projectdiscovery/chaos-client/cmd/chaos@latest`. Further invocations from upstream: `chaos -h`; `export PDCP_API_KEY=$KFIOSA_SECRET`. Go client to communicate with Chaos dataset API.

name: chaos-client
summary: Go client to communicate with Chaos dataset API.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need recon, use chaos-client because Go client to communicate with Chaos dataset API.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `go install -v github.com/projectdiscovery/chaos-client/cmd/chaos@latest` as documented upstream for chaos-client.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `chaos -h` as documented upstream for chaos-client.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `export PDCP_API_KEY=$KFIOSA_SECRET` as documented upstream for chaos-client.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `chaos -d uber.com -silent` as documented upstream for chaos-client.
When chaining recon tools, pair chaos-client with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `chaos-client --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
